description: ExposoGraph 3.0 Phase 5: Biologically-meaningful execution scenarios drawn from EG2.0 data. Each scenario specifies a canonical carcinogen class (resolved by aliases.py to the correct vocabulary key per module), tissue, genotype profile, and exposure scenario used to run the EXPOSURE→FLUX→MUTSIG chain.

scenarios.label: PAH_Lung_CYP1A1UM_GSTM1null_Smoking
scenarios.description: Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP) exposure in Lung tissue. High-risk genotype: CYP1A1 ultra-metabolizer (CYP1A1*1F inducible phenotype, ~1.8x baseline) plus GSTM1-null (complete gene deletion, ~50% population frequency). Active smoker (1 pack/day). EXPOSURE class PAH resolves directly to PAH in all three modules. Expected: dramatically elevated activation flux; near-zero detoxification (GSTM1-null eliminates primary BPDE conjugation); very high flux_ratio; SBS4 dominant (tobacco PAH signature).
scenarios.expected_qualitative: Very high activation flux; GSTM1-null eliminates detox; flux_ratio >> 1; SBS4 dominant (C>A transversions)
scenarios.biological_rationale: CYP1A1 UM genotype (CYP1A1*1F/*1F or high-inducibility phenotype) is the primary BaP activator in lung. GSTM1-null prevents BPDE-GSH conjugation. Combined effect is a very high activation-to-detox ratio. Smoker scenario gives [S]=0.015 uM (NHANES-derived). Sources: Shimada et al. 1996; GSTM1 null polymorphism literature; NHANES.

scenarios.label: Aflatoxin_Liver_CYP3A4NM_GSTM1active_HighEndemicDiet
scenarios.description: Aflatoxin B1 (AFB1) exposure in Liver tissue. Normal-activity genotypes: CYP3A4 NM + CYP1A2 NM (primary activators) + GSTM1 active (detox competent). High endemic diet (developing country, [S]=0.05 uM estimated liver concentration). Canonical class 'Aflatoxin' resolves to AflatoxinB1 in EXPOSURE, Aflatoxin in FLUX, Aflatoxin in MUTSIG. Expected: moderate-to-high activation (CYP3A4 Km=49.6 uM, Vmax=43.6; CYP1A2 Km=58.2 uM, Vmax=283.0); partial detoxification by CYP3A4_AFQ1; SBS24 dominant.
scenarios.expected_qualitative: Moderate-to-high activation flux; partial detox via CYP3A4 AFQ1 pathway; flux_ratio ~10-20; SBS24 dominant (C>A at CpG in liver)
scenarios.biological_rationale: AFB1 is activated by CYP3A4 (Km=49.6 uM, Vmax=43.6 pmol/min/pmol P450) and CYP1A2 (Km=58.2 uM, Vmax=283.0) in liver. Detox via CYP3A4-mediated AFQ1 hydroxylation (Km=160 uM, Vmax=67.0). GSTA1 Vmax is missing from EG2.0 records — surfaced as None. Full Km/Vmax data from PMC9469084 (Lootens et al. 2022) and PMID:8975785.

scenarios.label: AromaticAmines_Bladder_NAT2slow_Smoker
scenarios.description: Aromatic amine (4-aminobiphenyl, 4-ABP) exposure in Bladder tissue. Canonical class 'AromaticAmines' resolves to EXPOSURE=AromaticAmines/smoker ([S]=0.05 uM), FLUX=HCA (shares CYP1A2 N-hydroxylation / NAT2 N-acetylation mechanism), MUTSIG=AromaticAmines. NAT2 slow acetylator (multiplier=0.3) reduces competing N-acetylation detoxification. CYP1A2 NM is the primary activator. Expected: activation via CYP1A2/CYP1A1 (CLint proxy Km/Vmax); NAT2 slow reduces competing acetylation (NAT2 Km missing from EG2.0 data); SBS29 dominant (aromatic amine signature in bladder).
scenarios.expected_qualitative: Moderate activation (CYP1A2/CYP1A1 N-hydroxylation via CLint proxy); NAT2 slow reduces competing acetylation (Km missing); flux_ratio > 1; SBS29 dominant (aromatic amine/bladder signature)
scenarios.biological_rationale: 4-ABP and HCA (PhIP) share N-hydroxylation by CYP1A2 (Km=79 uM, CLint=1.14) and CYP1A1 (Km=5.1 uM, CLint=3.14). NAT2 slow acetylator (activity_multiplier=0.3) fails to acetylate N-OH-PhIP/4-ABP efficiently (competing detox). SULT1A1 and NAT2 Km values are missing from EG2.0 parameter records — surfaced as None. AromaticAmines EXPOSURE class provides smoker [S]=0.05 uM (tobacco is major 4-ABP source). Sources: PMC1829392; NHANES IARC Vol 99.

scenarios.label: Nitrosamine_Liver_CYP2A13NM_DietaryNitroso_HighProcessedMeat
scenarios.description: N-nitrosamines in Liver tissue. Canonical class 'Nitrosamine' resolves to EXPOSURE=DietaryNitroso/high_processed_meat ([S]=0.05 uM; chronic high processed-meat / alcohol co-exposure context), FLUX=Nitrosamine (CYP2A13, Km=3.6 uM, Vmax=13.8), MUTSIG=Nitrosamine. CYP2A13 NM is the principal nitrosamine activator. GSTM1 and GSTT1 active but no quantifiable FLUX detox Km/Vmax (carbonyl reduction and CYP2A6 both have missing params in EG2.0). Expected: significant NNK/NDMA activation in liver; detox surfaced as None/zero; SBS29+SBS11 dominant.
scenarios.expected_qualitative: Significant nitrosamine activation (CYP2A13 Km=3.6 uM; high efficiency); no quantifiable detox (carbonyl reduction Km/Vmax missing from EG2.0); flux_ratio >> 1; SBS29 and SBS11 dominant
scenarios.biological_rationale: Dietary nitrosamines (e.g. NDMA from processed meat) are activated by CYP2A13 (Km=3.6 uM, Vmax=13.8 pmol/min/pmol P450, PMID:12975327) in liver. CYP2A13 NM standard activity multiplier=1.0 (standard scale). DietaryNitroso/high_processed_meat scenario gives [S]=0.05 uM. GSTM1 and GSTT1 are active but no FLUX detox parameters for Nitrosamine class with real Km/Vmax. Sources: PMID:12975327; DietaryNitroso EXPOSURE class.
